scanner_blind_spots.reason: Scanner missed because VRT recently crossed $100B cap and is classified as industrials/electrical — scanner's sector model doesn't cross-reference it with AI infrastructure thesis. It's the single most important picks-and-shovels AI play with $15B+ backlog and 51% EPS growth.

Utility sector classification — scanner doesn't scan utilities for AI data center power demand thesis. Tepper doubling to 5th-largest holding is a 13F cross-sector signal that single-sector scanners miss. 14.8x Fwd P/E for 85% EBITDA growth is a structural scanner gap.

Revenue declining -1.3% auto-excludes from momentum scanners. But 34 insider purchases / $15.4M / zero sales over 6 months from a director with ~$197M position is a multi-month accumulation pattern invisible to event-driven single-transaction scanners.

Revenue declining -6.5% on DOGE = automatic exclusion from most scanner momentum filters. But $38B backlog + 9.1x Fwd P/E + 47.8% drawdown + DOGE fear peaking is a recovery setup that requires cross-referencing geopolitical context with valuation — a scanner can't do this.

NVIDIA's $2B investment in March 2026 reclassified LITE from telecom photonics to AI infrastructure supplier overnight. The scanner's category model based on historical SIC codes didn't capture the categorical re-rating. By June 2026 LITE is a completely different company than it was classified as.

No scanner tracks $10M+ director cluster buying in a $1.1B micro-cap food distribution company. Reading Form 4s across tiny-cap companies with 4 covering analysts requires exactly the kind of human/AI discovery that automated scanners don't prioritize.
